title: FR-EVT-* event ID registry + standardization (typed event taxonomy)
goal: Land the canonical FR-EVT-* event ID registry as a typed Rust enum + JSON manifest so every cluster X emission (mailbox backpressure, MT cancellation, checkpoint overflow, span lifecycle, replay phases, escalation, starvation) uses a registered ID instead of a free-form string. Eliminates the silent typo / drift risk that plagues string-based event taxonomies.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/observability/fr_events.rs with `FrEventId` enum + `FrEventRegistry`; new JSON manifest .GOV/roles_shared/records/FR_EVENT_REGISTRY.json; unit tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/fr_event_registry_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-196 span primitives merged so FR-EVT-SPAN-* events can be bound to span types.
implementation_notes: Define `FrEventId` enum with one variant per registered ID. Initial taxonomy covers cluster X emissions: `MailboxBackpressure`, `MailboxRoutingDenied`, `MailboxLeaseAcquired`, `MailboxLeaseExpired`, `MailboxLeaseTakeover`, `MtCancelRequested`, `MtCancelForced`, `MtCancelCleanupFailed`, `MtStarved`, `MtExecError`, `Mt015DistillationCandidate` (alias for FR-EVT-MT-015 from folded stub), `CheckpointOverflow`, `CheckpointShutdownForced`, `ReplayStarted`, `ReplayProgress`, `ReplayCompleted`, `ReplayFailed`, `RestartResumeStarted`, `RestartResumeSessionResumed`, `RestartResumeSessionRecoveryFailed`, `RestartResumeDbUnavailable`, `RestartResumeCompleted`, `SpanStarted`, `SpanEnded`, `SpanFailed`. Each variant maps to a canonical string ID like `FR-EVT-MAILBOX-BACKPRESSURE`, retrievable via `FrEventId::as_str() -> &'static str` and `FrEventId::from_str(s) -> Result<FrEventId, UnknownEventId>`. JSON manifest at .GOV/roles_shared/records/FR_EVENT_REGISTRY.json mirrors the enum: `{ schema: "handshake.fr_event_registry@1", version: "1.0.0", events: [ { id: "FR-EVT-MAILBOX-BACKPRESSURE", kind: "emission", subsystem: "role_mailbox", added_in_wp: "WP-KERNEL-004", schema_fields: [{ name: "role_id", kind: "string" }, ...] }, ... ] }`. A unit test asserts Rust enum + JSON manifest are byte-byte aligned (every enum variant has a JSON entry and vice versa). Every cluster X site that previously had a string literal for an FR event ID MUST switch to `FrEventId::MtCancelRequested.as_str()` (this is a refactor obligation propagated to MT-182/186/188/189/191/192/193/195 â€” the registry MT lands the type and the others adopt it on their own MT). Tests: (a) round-trip enum<->string for every variant; (b) JSON manifest parse + schema check; (c) Rust + JSON alignment test enumerates both sides and asserts equality; (d) unknown event ID round-trip returns UnknownEventId error.
risk_if_missed: Without a typed registry, every site emits free-form strings; typos slip in (FR-EVT-MAILBOX-BACKPRESURE vs FR-EVT-MAILBOX-BACKPRESSURE); diagnostics panel filters break silently; the folded stub's 'new FR event families' invariant becomes a string-soup.
validator_focus: Rust enum and JSON manifest provably aligned (test fails if either drifts); every variant has a documented schema_fields description; .GOV/roles_shared/records/ JSON validated via JSON Schema (forward-compat for HBR-INT-001 registry pattern).

minimum_controls: Rust-JSON alignment test fails CI if either side drifts (no manual sync trust).
JSON manifest validated via JSON Schema (added to .GOV/roles_shared/records/ schema dir).
Adopters in MT-182/186/188/189/191/192/193/195 noted as refactor obligations; each MT picks up FrEventId on its own implementation.
